items.title: Network Security Basics for Modern Teams
items.excerpt: A practical overview of the controls that help modern organizations reduce risk, protect users, and maintain visibility across the network.
items.content: Modern network security starts with clear segmentation, strong access controls, and continuous monitoring. Teams should align firewall policies, identity checks, and endpoint protection so threats are contained before they spread. This dummy article is meant to test article filtering and card layout in the insight section.
items.author: AMI Security Team

items.title: Zero Trust Explained in Simple Terms
items.excerpt: A straightforward dummy article that explains the core idea behind Zero Trust and why it matters for distributed work environments.
items.content: Zero Trust is based on the principle of verifying every request instead of assuming trust inside the network. In practice, this means stronger identity checks, limited access, and continuous validation of devices and users. The content here is intentionally generic so it can serve as safe placeholder data in the insight page.
items.author: AMI Security Team

items.title: Planning a Scalable IT Infrastructure
items.excerpt: A dummy editorial about designing infrastructure that can grow with user demand, office expansion, and new application workloads.
items.content: A scalable infrastructure plan considers capacity, resilience, and operational simplicity from the beginning. Good architecture avoids single points of failure, keeps core services observable, and leaves room for future growth. This placeholder content helps validate the article list without using production copy.
items.author: AMI Infrastructure Team

items.title: Bandwidth and QoS Principles
items.excerpt: A dummy explanation of traffic prioritization, queue management, and performance tuning for busy enterprise networks.
items.content: QoS helps prioritize critical applications when network demand becomes uneven. By classifying traffic and reserving capacity for important services, teams can reduce congestion and improve user experience. This article is intentionally generic and works as a placeholder for testing repeated category entries.

items.content: Technology teams are increasingly adopting automation to reduce repetitive tasks and improve response time. AI-assisted monitoring, edge processing, and software-defined services are becoming common parts of modern IT roadmaps. items.title: Cloud Readiness Checklist for Growing Companies
items.excerpt: A practical dummy checklist to help teams think through migration planning, governance, and operational readiness before moving workloads.
items.content: Before a cloud migration, teams should review application dependencies, identity controls, backup strategy, and cost visibility. Clear ownership and phased adoption reduce risk and make the transition easier to manage. This placeholder article helps test a fifth category in the UI.

items.title: IT Security Spending to Reach $96 Billion in 2018
items.excerpt: Gartner predicts that worldwide security spend will reach $96 billion dollars, up 8% from the previous year. The latest figures show substantial increases reflecting the growing importance of cybersecurity in modern enterprises.
items.content: Global security spending continues to grow as organizations respond to more advanced threats, stricter compliance needs, and wider digital adoption across industries. Enterprises are allocating more budget to endpoint protection, network security, monitoring, and incident response capabilities to reduce operational risk. For businesses in Indonesia, this trend reinforces the need for a layered security strategy that can scale with evolving threat landscapes.

items.title: The Real Reason You're Failing at PCI DSS Compliance
items.excerpt: IT organizations don't struggle with PCI DSS compliance due to a lack of knowledge or technology. The problem is proficiency. With years of data breach investigation reports, patterns start to become clear.
items.content: PCI DSS compliance often fails not because the standard is unclear, but because teams lack consistent processes and operational discipline. Organizations need to map technical controls to daily practices, ensure documentation is maintained, and regularly test their security posture. A mature compliance program combines policy, training, monitoring, and audit readiness so security becomes part of the workflow instead of a one-time project.

items.title: DDI is a Critical Component of IoT Success
items.excerpt: The Internet of Things era has arrived, and businesses need to be prepared for a world where everything is connected. There are currently 25 billion internet-connected devices, and that will double by 2025.
items.content: As IoT deployments expand, organizations need a reliable foundation for DNS, DHCP, and IP address management to keep devices connected and visible. DDI helps teams control scale, reduce configuration errors, and improve troubleshooting across distributed environments. Without a solid DDI strategy, the complexity of connected devices can quickly overwhelm network operations and security teams.

items.title: Fighting Automation with Automation
items.excerpt: Disruptions caused by autonomous malware could have devastating implications. As attackers leverage automation, defenders must also adopt automated threat detection and response capabilities to keep pace.
items.content: Automation has become a double-edged sword in cybersecurity. Attackers use it to scale phishing, reconnaissance, and malware delivery, while defenders can use the same principles to accelerate detection, triage, and response. Security teams that invest in automated workflows can reduce dwell time, react faster to incidents, and improve consistency across their defenses.

items.title: AMI Renews Strategic Partnership with Juniper Networks
items.excerpt: Artha Mitra Interdata proudly announces the renewal of its strategic partnership with Juniper Networks, reinforcing our commitment to delivering cutting-edge networking solutions to clients across Indonesia.
items.content: The renewed partnership with Juniper Networks reflects AMI's ongoing commitment to delivering reliable networking solutions and expert implementation support for clients in Indonesia. This collaboration strengthens our ability to provide modern infrastructure, security, and performance-focused technologies that help organizations grow. Through this partnership, customers can expect continued access to trusted solutions and technical expertise.

items.title: Ransomware Threats Continue to Rise in Southeast Asia
items.excerpt: Southeast Asian enterprises face an increasing wave of ransomware attacks targeting critical infrastructure. Organizations are urged to strengthen their security posture with multi-layered defense strategies.
items.content: Ransomware remains one of the most disruptive threats facing enterprises in Southeast Asia, targeting critical systems, backups, and user credentials. Organizations need a multi-layered defense that includes strong endpoint protection, network segmentation, identity controls, and tested recovery plans. Proactive monitoring and employee awareness are essential to reducing both the likelihood and impact of an attack.
